records.ety: [OE. tresor, tresour, F. trsor, L. thesaurus, Gr. <?/ a stone laid up, treasure, probably from the root of <?/ to put, place. See Thesis, and cf. Thesaurus.]

defs: Wealth accumulated; especially, a stock, or store of money in reserve.
quotes: This treasure hath fortune unto us given.

defs: A great quantity of anything collected for future use; abundance; plenty.
quotes: We have treasures in the field, of wheat and of barley, and of oil and of honey.

defs: That which is very much valued.
quotes: Ye shall be peculiar treasure unto me.
From thy wardrobe bring thy chiefest treasure.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Treasured (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Treasuring.]

defs: To collect and deposit, as money or other valuable things, for future use; to lay up; to hoard; usually with up; as, to treasure up gold.
